titleEn: To ask or give information about projects

conceptExplanation.en: OF36 ŌĆö To ask or give information about projects. This objective develops the language needed to ask or give information about projects in a Government of Canada workplace. Grammar focus: The present tense, The simple future. Study the PFL2 source vocabulary (panel below), review the grammar notes, then practise and consolidate with workplace tasks.

grammarNotes.summary: Grammar focus for this objective: The present tense, The simple future.
points: Le pr├®sent de lŌĆÖindicatif (The present tense) ŌĆö Exprime des faits, des habitudes et des situations actuelles. Les verbes r├®guliers en -er/-ir/-re ont des terminaisons fixes; ├¬tre, avoir, aller, faire sont irr├®guliers.
┬Ę ├¬tre : suis, es, est, sommes, ├¬tes, sont. avoir : ai, as, a, avons, avez, ont.

Le futur simple (The simple future) ŌĆö Faits et intentions futurs. Terminaisons -ai, -as, -a, -ons, -ez, -ont sur lŌĆÖinfinitif. Radicaux irr├®guliers : ├¬treŌåÆser-, avoirŌåÆaur-, allerŌåÆir-, faireŌåÆfer-.
┬Ę Endings added to the infinitive (or stem): -ai, -as, -a, -ons, -ez, -ont.
┬Ę -re verbs drop the final -e: prendre ŌåÆ prendr-.
┬Ę Irregular stems: ser-, aur-, ir-, fer-, viendr-, pourr-, devr-.
Listen to and repeat the source vocabulary aloud; French final consonants are often silent and vowels are pure.
Liaison links a final consonant to a following vowel (e.g. vousŌĆ┐├¬tes, nousŌĆ┐avons).
